iti27:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti27:2.1:
“Monks, all the grounds for making merit leading to spontaneously arising [in
heaven] do not equal one-sixteenth of the awareness-release through good will.
iti27:3.1:
“Just as the radiance of all the stars does not equal one-sixteenth of the radiance of the moon,
as the moon–surpassing them–shines, blazes, & dazzles,
iti27:3.2:
even so, all the grounds for making merit leading to spontaneously arising [in
heaven] do not equal one-sixteenth of the awareness-release through good will.
Good will–surpassing them–shines, blazes, & dazzles.
iti27:4.1:
“Just as in the last month of the rains, in autumn, when the sky is clear & cloudless, the sun,
on ascending the sky, overpowers the space immersed in darkness, shines, blazes, & dazzles, even so,
iti27:4.2:
all the grounds for making merit leading to spontaneously arising [in heaven]
do not equal one-sixteenth of the awareness-release through good will.
Good will–surpassing them–shines, blazes, & dazzles.
iti27:5.1: “Just as in the last stage of the night the morning star shines, blazes, & dazzles, even so,
iti27:5.2:
all the grounds for making merit leading to spontaneously arising [in heaven]
do not equal one-sixteenth of the awareness-release through good will.
Good will–surpassing them–shines, blazes, & dazzles.”
iti27:6.4: on seeing the ending of acquisitions.
iti27:7.1: If with uncorrupted mind you feel good will for even one being,
iti27:7.3: But a noble one produces a mind of sympathy for all beings,
iti27:8.1: Kingly seers, who conquered the earth swarming with beings,
iti27:9.1: but these don’t equal one sixteenth
iti27:9.2: of a well-developed mind of good will–
iti27:9.3: as all the constellations don’t, one sixteenth of the radiance of the moon.
iti27:10.1: One who neither kills nor gets others to kill,
iti27:10.2: neither conquers, nor gets others to conquer,
iti27:10.3: with good will for all beings,
iti27:10.4: has no hostility with anyone at all.
